seo_title: Handcrafted Bronze Antler Jewelry | Moon Raven Designs
meta_description: Bronze antler pendants and stag jewelry, cast on Vancouver Island. Lost-wax cast bronze with deep symbolism from Celtic and Norse traditions.
intro: The antler has long marked the boundary between the wild and the human, a symbol of strength and regeneration. In bronze, that boundary becomes tangible — the metal's earthy weight and warm glow echo the forest floor, the antler's branching form reaching skyward. This pairing is for those who carry a piece of the wild with them, who feel the pull of ancient groves and the stories carved into bone and metal.
why_this_pairing: Bronze holds the memory of fire and earth. When cast into the shape of an antler, it captures the tension between growth and decay — the stag sheds its crown each year only to regrow it, and bronze develops its own living patina over time. The metal's density gives the pendant a satisfying heft, grounding the symbol of the antler in a way that lighter metals cannot. The warm, reddish-brown tone of bronze complements the organic lines of antler designs, making the piece feel as though it was unearthed from an ancient burial mound rather than fabricated in a modern studio. For the wearer, this pairing speaks to resilience, the cycles of nature, and the quiet power of the wild.
about_the_material: Our bronze is a solid copper alloy, heavier than sterling silver and with a distinctly warm tone. Each piece is cast using the lost-wax method, a process that preserves every fine detail of the original carving. Bronze develops a natural patina over time — a darkening that many prefer for its aged, rustic character. If you wish to maintain the original shine, simply polish with a soft cloth. Bronze is generally safe for sensitive skin, though some may react to the copper content; a clear lacquer can be applied upon request.
about_the_symbol: The antler and the stag carry deep meaning across many cultures. In Celtic tradition, the stag is associated with Cernunnos, the horned god of the forest, embodying fertility, wildness, and the untamed spirit. In Norse mythology, four stags graze on the branches of Yggdrasill, the world tree, representing the cycle of life and the connection between the heavens and the earth. The antler itself — shed and regrown each year — has long been a symbol of renewal, strength, and the enduring cycles of nature. For many, wearing an antler is a way to honor that wildness within.
how_to_wear: A bronze antler pendant makes a statement on its own, worn close to the collarbone on a simple chain. For a more layered look, pair it with other nature-inspired pieces in bronze or leather — avoid mixing with bright silver if you want to keep the palette rustic. The pendant's weight suits a 20- to 24-inch chain for men or those who prefer a lower drape; a shorter 18-inch chain works well for a more delicate profile. This piece transitions easily from a flannel shirt to a linen tunic, from a forest walk to an evening gathering.
care: To keep your bronze antler jewelry looking its best, avoid contact with chlorine, salt water, and harsh household cleaners. Over time, the bronze will naturally darken and develop a patina; if you prefer the original warm glow, buff it gently with a jewelry polishing cloth. For deep cleaning, use mild soap and water, then dry thoroughly. Store your pendant away from direct sunlight and humidity. If the patina becomes uneven or you need a professional refresh, bring it to your jeweler.

faq.q: What does an antler symbolize in jewelry?
faq.a: Antlers symbolize strength, renewal, and connection to the wild. They are often associated with the stag, which represents leadership, fertility, and the cycles of nature in Celtic and Norse traditions.

faq.q: How do you care for bronze jewelry?
faq.a: Clean bronze with a soft cloth and mild soap and water. Avoid chlorine, salt water, and household cleaners. Over time, bronze develops a natural patina; polish if you prefer a brighter finish.

faq.q: What is the difference between white bronze and yellow bronze?
faq.a: White bronze is a copper alloy with tin and sometimes nickel, giving it a silvery appearance. Yellow bronze has a higher copper content, resulting in a warm, golden-brown tone. Our bronze is a traditional yellow bronze.

faq.q: Is bronze jewelry safe for sensitive skin?
faq.a: Bronze contains copper, which can cause a greenish discoloration on skin for some people. This is not harmful and washes off easily. For those with very sensitive skin, a clear lacquer coating can be applied.

Our bronze antler pieces are created using the lost-wax casting method. A wax model is carved, encased in a ceramic shell, then heated to remove the wax. Molten bronze is poured into the cavity, and after cooling, the shell is broken away to reveal the metal form.

faq.q: What does a stag represent in mythology?
faq.a: In Celtic mythology, the stag is linked to Cernunnos, the horned god of the forest, symbolizing wildness and fertility. In Norse myth, stags graze on Yggdrasill, representing the connection between worlds.

faq.q: How do you choose the right necklace length for a pendant?
faq.a: For a bronze antler pendant, an 18-inch chain sits at the collarbone, ideal for a focal piece. A 20- to 24-inch chain allows the pendant to hang lower, suitable for layering or for a more casual look.

faq.q: What is the meaning of a deer skull in jewelry?
faq.a: A deer skull, often with antlers, symbolizes mortality, the cycle of life and death, and the raw aspects of nature. It is a reminder of the wild's untamed power and the beauty in impermanence.
